text: A man using his own ribs as the rungs of a ladder.
A dinner plate where the food is screaming back at the diner.
A woman unzipping her skin to reveal a second, identical woman.
A cityscape where the buildings are made of giant, stacked teeth.
A businessman's necktie that turns into a constrictor snake.
A pair of eyes floating in a bowl of soup like meatballs.
A telephone handset that is a human ear on both ends.
A tree where the leaves are tiny, reaching hands.
A man trying to shave, but his reflection is refusing to move.
A bird's nest made entirely of human hair and jewelry.
A suitcase that, when opened, contains a miniature, crowded theater.
A teapot pouring out thick, black shadows instead of tea.
A hand with a mouth in the palm, biting its own fingers.
A person's shadow detaching itself to walk in the opposite direction.
A bouquet of flowers where the centers are staring eyeballs.
A man sewing his own shadow back onto his heels.
A clock where the hands are long, pointed fingers.
A face where the nose has been replaced by a smaller, screaming face.
A bathtub overflowing with letters that have never been read.
A person wearing a mask that is more realistic than their actual face.
A chair that has human legs and is trying to walk away.
A typewriter where the keys are individual human teeth.
A man looking into a mirror and seeing his internal organs.
A staircase that leads into a giant, open mouth.
A sky where the clouds are shaped like various internal organs.
A pair of scissors cutting through the fabric of the sky.
A person whose umbrella is raining on them from the inside.
A loaf of bread that contains a hidden, sleeping infant.
A man's brain being sliced like a block of cheese.
A dollhouse where the dolls are performing a complex surgery.
A person's footprints filling up with dark, murky water.
A lightbulb that emits darkness, turning the room pitch black.
A woman's dress made entirely of living butterflies.
A man's beard that has become a tangled web of spiders.
A window that looks out onto a different century.
A person whose reflection is several seconds behind their movements.
A musical instrument that produces visible, solid shapes instead of sound.
A dinner party where all the guests are wearing paper bags over their heads.
A man's shadow being stepped on, causing him physical pain.
A bed that is actually a giant, open book.
A person's thoughts escaping their head as colorful, toxic smoke.
A mirror that shows the viewer as they will look in fifty years.
A bird with human arms instead of wings.
A person whose skin is covered in delicate, intricate maps.
A fountain that is flowing with black ink.
A man trying to catch his own head as it rolls away.
A house where the doors and windows are constantly shifting positions.
A person whose veins are visible and arranged like a city map.
A giant eye watching a small, oblivious crowd from the horizon.
A man melting into a puddle, leaving only his hat and glasses behind.
A refrigerator that, when opened, reveals a brick wall instead of food.
A window shade that shows a different room inside when pulled down.
A coffee mug where the handle is a curled, tensed human finger.
A standard floor lamp where the lightbulb is replaced by an unblinking tongue.
A dinner fork with tines made of long, manicured fingernails.
A desk calendar where every single date is stamped with the word 'YESTERDAY'.
A bar of soap that grows human hair the more it is used.
A ceiling fan where the blades are flat, spinning neckties.
A wall clock that melts down the wall like grease when someone looks at it.
A closet full of hangers holding empty, hollow shapes of suits without fabric.
A vacuum cleaner that exhales a dense layer of dust onto clean carpets.
A doorknob that grows a set of teeth when a hand approaches it.
A window pane that cracks into the precise pattern of a spiderweb when a bird flies past.
A standard pillow filled with heavy, rattling pebbles instead of feathers.
A stack of pancakes oozing thick, black tar instead of syrup.
A mirror that only reflects the back of the person standing in front of it.
A wooden rocking chair that rocks violently only when empty.
A picture frame holding a portrait that slowly turns its back to the viewer.
A key that twists into a corkscrew shape the moment it enters a lock.
A wine bottle pouring out solid, brittle glass shards.
A comb with rows of miniature human teeth instead of plastic bristles.
A table lamp whose shadow on the wall resembles a crouching predatory bird.
A typewriter where every keystroke punches a clean hole through the paper.
A closed door with a keyhole that drips a single teardrop every hour.
A kitchen sponge shaped like a small, sleeping lung.
A standard envelope that bites the finger of anyone attempting to open it.
A desk drawer filled to the brim with loose, discarded human fingernails.
A teapot with two spouts pouring liquid in completely opposite directions.
A pair of leather shoes that face away from each other when placed side-by-side.
A toothbrush with sharp, metallic needles instead of soft bristles.
A wall mirror that acts like water, rippling when a finger touches its surface.
A bowl of cereal where the individual pieces are tiny, floating wedding rings.
A ceiling light fixture that hangs down like a heavy, industrial plumb bob.
A television screen showing a live broadcast of an empty chair in an empty room.
A salt shaker that dispenses fine, dark ashes instead of salt.
A staircase where every individual step is slightly narrower than the last.
A kitchen knife that bends like soft rubber whenever it touches food.
A clothes iron that leaves a charred, silhouette footprint on clothing.
A coat rack where the hooks are shaped like skeletal, reaching hands.
A writing desk with legs that taper off into sharp, metallic spikes.
A matchstick that burns with a cold, solid black flame.
A book whose pages are glued together around a hollowed-out, glass eye.
A wall clock with numbers that have all pooled down to the bottom of the face.
A bar stool that slowly sinks into the floor like quicksand under weight.
A coffee pot brewing a thick, opaque layer of red ink.
A window frame that tightly pinches shut like an eyelid.
A pencil that writes in a texture resembling raised, surgical scars.
A ceiling that is sagging downward like a heavy, water-logged mattress.
A leather briefcase that gasps for air whenever the latches are popped.
A window looking directly into a solid, unyielding brick wall.
A wall calendar where the grid of dates is a labyrinth with no exit.
A teacup with an internal partition that prevents it from ever being filled.
A dining table supported by four massive, unmoving human thumbs.
A pair of eyeglasses with opaque, solid marble spheres instead of lenses.
A safety pin fastening together two separate flaps of living skin.
A wrist watch where the ticking mechanism mimics a rapid, nervous heartbeat.
A slice of toast showing a burnt silhouette of a padlock.
A bathroom sink where the drain is a tiny, spiraling whirlpool of hair.
A standard thimble made from a hollowed-out human tooth.
A picture frame displaying nothing but a beautifully painted shadow.
A window string that ties itself into a neat hangman's noose.
A bookshelf where the volumes are bound in coarse, sand-paper texturing.
A pair of scissors where the blades are two profile faces kissing.
A plate of spaghetti where the noodles are tightly knotted ballpoint wires.
A standard coin that has a screaming face stamped on both sides.
A floor rug that has a distinct, rising human pulse beneath it.
A light switch that pricks the user's finger every time it is toggled.
A bar of soap with a rusted razor blade safely embedded dead-center.
A water glass filled with a dense, unyielding block of clear ice.
A wooden wardrobe that hums a low, discordant tone when closed.
A man's hand where each knuckle is an independent, staring eye.
A person whose torso is a birdcage housing a silent, featherless crow.
A profile of a woman whose braid is made entirely of twisted copper wire.
A man unbuttoning his own chest coat to reveal a hollow, empty cavity.
A pair of legs walking down the street with a rolling suitcase but no torso.
A human skull where the jawbone is replaced by a heavy brass zipper.
A person whose tongue has grown long enough to be tied into a necktie.
A figure whose throat is a clear, hollow glass tube showing swallowed keys.
A man peeling off his own fingerprints like small stickers.
A woman's face where the lips are sewn shut with thick, coarse twine.
A human foot where the toes are arranged in a perfect, closed circle.
A person whose ears are replaced by miniature, tightly closed fists.
A torso wrapped tightly in layers of heavy, restrictive measuring tape.
A man's arm that hinges backward at the elbow like a praying mantis.
A figure whose backbone is visible on the outside, resembling a zipper.
A person attempting to brush their teeth, but their gums are solid bone.
A man whose head is completely hollow, acting as an inkwell for a quill.
A face where the eyes have migrated down to sit on the cheekbones.
A woman whose long hair is woven directly into the fabric of her chair.
A hand with an extra joint in every finger, folding like an accordion.
A man's neck that has elongated and twisted twice like a rope.
A figure whose chest features a small, functional peephole with a cover.
A person whose skin is peeling back to reveal a layer of legal text.
A man holding his own heart in his palm like a pocket watch.
A human mouth where the teeth are tiny, sharp, vertical filing cabinets.
A figure whose limbs are jointed like a wooden artist's mannequin.
A man shaving his face, only to reveal a layer of solid clockwork beneath.
A person whose fingers taper off into long, functional sewing needles.
A torso that splits down the middle to reveal a small, interior staircase.
A man with a second, smaller pair of hands emerging from his collarbones.
A human eye with a tiny, working keyhole directly in the pupil.
A figure whose head is entirely wrapped in heavy, thick bandages.
A person whose lungs are visible through a transparent, glass chest wall.
A man whose jaw opens wide enough to swallow his own fist.
A hand where the thumb is replaced by a small, working fountain pen nib.
A person whose skin has the texture and grain of old, dry timber.
A man pulling a long, endless thread out from an open seam in his wrist.
A face where the eyebrows are two caterpillars crawling away.
A human torso constructed out of stacked, leatherbound ledger books.
A figure whose legs are fused into a single, solid architectural pillar.
A person whose fingernails have grown into small, pointed mirrors.
A man trying to blow out a candle, but his breath is a solid spike.
A hand where the lines of the palm are deep, bleeding trenches.
A figure whose head is attached to their shoulders by a heavy hinge.
A person whose ribcage has been converted into a small filing system.
A man's face slowly melting down into his starched white collar.
A human arm that ends in a heavy, solid wooden mallet.
A figure whose ears are large, spiraled sea shells.
A person whose shadow reveals an internal anatomy they do not possess.
A man's mouth open to reveal a tiny, miniature version of himself inside.
A foot where the heel has been replaced by a heavy iron wheel.
A person whose collarbones are shaped like a wishbone about to snap.
A hand clasping another hand, but both belong to the same arm.
A figure whose stomach is a hollow alcove holding a ticking metronome.
A man's head neatly sliced into three vertical, offset sections.
A human tongue textured exactly like a rough piece of sandpaper.
A person whose spine is a heavy, rusted iron chain.
A face where the eyes are tightly closed, but the eyelids have eyes painted on them.
A man's fist enclosed within a second, larger translucent fist.
A figure whose skin is cracked and crazed like old porcelain.
A man's shadow on the wall holding a weapon he does not possess.
A person walking down an alley where all the shadows point toward them.
A man looking into a mirror and seeing his reflection weeping silently.
A shadow that remains standing upright after the person has sat down.
Two men sitting across from each other, both holding the other's leash.
A person whose shadow is attached to the ceiling instead of the floor.
A man looking at a silhouette of himself that has been cut out of the wall.
A figure surrounded by a circle of shadows that are slowly closing in.
A man shaking hands with his own reflection, which has reached out of the glass.
A shadow on the ground that has a completely different face than the subject.
A person sitting in a room where their shadow is the only thing cast in color.
A man whose shadow is wearing a crown while he wears a bowler hat.
A figure watching their own shadow run away down a dark street.
A mirror that shows a completely empty room despite the man standing before it.
A man's shadow that has been neatly folded up and left on a chair.
A person whose reflection is turning away in disgust from them.
A shadow that appears to be heavier than the person casting it, dragging them back.
A man looking into a pool and seeing his reflection drowning beneath the surface.
A figure whose shadow is made of a dense swarm of tiny ants.
A person standing between two mirrors, seeing an endless line of strangers.
A man's shadow stretching out across the floor to trip another passerby.
A shadow that possesses a distinct, sharp outline while the man is blurry.
A person whose reflection is pointing a finger back at them accusingly.
A man trying to escape a shadow that is slowly climbing up his own legs.
A figure whose shadow is a complex maze that leads to their feet.
A mirror cracked in a way that divides the face into two different ages.
A man's shadow that has been pinned to the wall with heavy iron spikes.
A person whose reflection is moving at half-speed compared to reality.
A shadow that appears to be whispering into the ear of the person casting it.
A man looking into a vanity mirror and seeing a brick wall behind him.
A figure whose shadow is completely transparent, revealing the floor beneath.
A person whose reflection has its eyes wide open while they are closed.
A man's shadow that has broken into three separate, independent silhouettes.
A shadow on the wall that is slowly peeling away from the surface like wallpaper.
A person whose reflection is trapped behind a row of iron bars.
A man looking at his shadow and noticing it has no head.
A shadow that remains cast on the wall long after the light is turned off.
A figure whose reflection is wearing a mask that the figure is not.
A man trying to sweep his own shadow into a dustpan.
A shadow that appears to be lifting a heavy weight off the ground on its own.
A person whose reflection is a skeletal frame in a business suit.
A man's shadow that is tangled up in the shadows of nearby furniture.
A mirror that reflects everything in the room except the walls.
A figure whose shadow is shaped like a large, ominous pair of shears.
A man looking into a pocket mirror and seeing a vast, open desert.
A shadow that is slowly consuming the light around it like an ink stain.
A person whose reflection is writing a letter on the inside of the glass.
A man's shadow that appears to be made of rough, unhewn stone.
A shadow that follows a step behind the person, mimicking their every hesitation.
A mirror that shows the viewer's face with all features completely smoothed over.
A man whose shadow is cast forward even though the light source is in front of him.
A figure whose reflection has turned its back and is walking away into the distance.
A shadow on the ground that appears to be a deep, bottomless trench.
A person whose reflection is melting like hot wax down the mirror.
A man's shadow that has been neatly ironed flat onto the pavement.
A shadow that appears to be pulling the man toward a dark corner.
A mirror that only reflects the skeletal structure of whatever is placed before it.
A figure whose shadow is a dense weave of heavy black thread.
A man looking at his reflection and realizing it is a photograph taped to the glass.
A shadow that detaches from the wall to lie flat on the floor like a rug.
A bureaucrat whose head is a giant rubber stamp, stamping his own chest.
A businessman climbing a mountain made entirely of discarded bowler hats.
A man sitting at a desk where the telephone is tethered directly to his ankle.
A row of identical men in suits, all connected by a single, shared necktie.
A judge whose gavel is a human fist made of solid stone.
A man carrying an enormous, heavy boulder that is shaped like a dollar sign.
A public monument where the statue is a man covering his face in shame.
A businessman whose briefcase is overflowing with sand that leaks onto the street.
A man sitting at a typewriter that is slowly typing out his own obituary.
A line of people waiting to enter a door that has been painted onto a solid wall.
A politician whose mouth is a megaphone made of living flesh.
A man trying to read a newspaper where all the text has run down into a puddle.
A bureaucrat whose desk has grown roots that anchor it deep into the floorboards.
A man in a suit whose shadow is a giant, menacing puppet on strings.
A factory assembly line where the products being made are small, wooden coffins.
A man wearing a suit of armor made entirely of legal documents and contracts.
A businessman whose head is a clock with no hands, only an empty face.
A line of identical men walking into a giant sausage grinder.
A man trying to catch a flying document that is out of his reach.
A bureaucrat whose fountain pen is a syringe drawing ink directly from his veins.
A man sitting in a theater where all the seats face away from the stage.
A businessman whose necktie is so long it trails behind him like a leash.
A man trying to open a safe using a key made of soft, melting wax.
A public square where the pavement is made of giant, flat gravestones.
A man in a suit whose body is a stack of cardboard boxes ready to collapse.
A bureaucrat signing a document with a feather quill that is on fire.
A man trying to scale a wall that is made of smooth, unyielding glass.
A businessman whose shadow is a giant, gaping mouth consuming his footsteps.
A line of people waiting for a bus that is a giant, hollow roller skate.
A man sitting at a desk that is slowly sinking into a pool of black ink.
A politician whose hands are replaced by two large, balancing scales.
A man trying to sweep up a pile of money that is turning into dead leaves.
A bureaucrat whose eyeglasses have been painted black from the inside.
A man in a suit whose head is a heavy iron padlock with no key.
A factory chimney belching out thick, black smoke in the shape of human profiles.
A man trying to fill a bucket that has a giant hole in the bottom.
A businessman whose coat pockets are filled with heavy, smooth river stones.
A line of people holding umbrellas that are all connected at the edges.
A man sitting at a dining table where the only dish is a large, iron key.
A bureaucrat whose chair is balanced precariously on a single, sharp nail.
A man trying to walk against a wind that is blowing letters and papers at his face.
A businessman whose shadow is a giant, menacing bird of prey.
A public park where all the benches face a solid brick wall.
A man trying to write on a piece of paper that is constantly rolling itself up.
A bureaucrat whose head is a giant ball of tangled red tape.
A man in a suit whose body is completely flat, like a cardboard cutout.
A factory worker whose hands have been replaced by heavy iron wrenches.
A man trying to open a door that has ten different locks but no handle.
A businessman whose briefcase is a birdcage containing a frantic, trapped pigeon.
A line of identical men all looking down into a deep, bottomless trench.
A politician whose tongue is a long, winding scroll of blank paper.
A man trying to balance a giant ledger book on the tip of his nose.
A bureaucrat whose desk is surrounded by a high, impenetrable wall of filing cabinets.
A man in a suit whose shadow is a giant pair of balances tipped completely to one side.
A factory where the smoke stacks are shaped like giant, grasping hands.
A man trying to read a book where all the letters are scattered at the bottom of the page.
A businessman whose coat is buttoned with small, round gears that are turning.
A line of people waiting to cross a bridge that terminates abruptly in mid-air.
A man sitting at a desk where the inkwell is a small, bottomless volcano.
A bureaucrat whose head is a heavy iron safe, tightly locked and bolted shut.
A small girl with enormous, solemn eyes presenting a single perfect strawberry to a bear in a top hat.
A rabbit dressed as a physician, listening to a cloud's heartbeat with a stethoscope.
A child discovering that their bedroom ceiling is a map of a country that does not exist.
A wide-eyed girl riding a very dignified tortoise through a supermarket.
A boy whose tears, when they hit the ground, immediately bloom into tiny flowers.
A child finding a door in a tree that opens onto a perfectly set dining table inside.
A small bear in a bow tie, sitting alone at a formal table, carefully eating a single pea.
A girl whose braids are so long that birds have built nests in them without her noticing.
A child carrying a jar of moonlight as though it were perfectly ordinary.
A tiny pig in suspenders, operating a very complex system of levers and pulleys to open a cookie jar.
A boy discovering that his shadow has secretly learned to juggle.
A child offering a daisy to a very bored-looking gargoyle on a cathedral ledge.
A small elephant wearing a nightgown, carrying a candle, looking for its lost thimble.
A child learning to sew clouds together from a very patient seamstress sparrow.
A boy whose dreams are so vivid that they spill out of his ears onto the pillow every morning.
A very small knight in tin-foil armor solemnly challenging a house cat to a duel.
A child whose pockets are always full of exactly the right number of buttons.
A fox in a housecoat, setting out seven small dishes of milk for the house spirits before bed.
A woman who tends a garden where all the vegetables are the past year's worries.
A stag whose antlers are a bare winter tree where small lights appear each December.
A group of elderly women riding geese across a full moon, arguing about the best route.
A cat who teaches astronomy to an owl, using a fallen acorn cap as a telescope.
A hedgehog postman delivering letters to creatures who live inside the roots of an oak tree.
A woman alchemist whose laboratory is cluttered with potions that turn silence into music.
A forest clearing where a circle of mushrooms serves as chairs for an ongoing debate between owls.
A river that sings lullabies only to the fish, keeping the fishermen awake with curiosity.
A grandmother whose knitting needles are weaving not wool but the local weather.
A snail who has been carrying the same grain of sand for forty years and considers it a treasure.
A bear who keeps a library of hibernation dreams, lending them out to insomniacs in spring.
A girl raised by foxes who is trying very hard to learn to knock on doors instead of scratch.
A woman who maps the seasonal migration of lost mittens across a city.
A crow who collects only the shadows of valuable things, keeping them in a velvet pouch.
A tortoise who has been delivering the same letter for thirty years and is not concerned about it.
A man in a bowler hat standing calmly inside a falling apple that is larger than the sky.
An ordinary Tuesday afternoon interrupted by a sky that has turned the color of a perfect peach.
A man discovering that all the doors in his house now open onto the same room.
A train that arrives exactly on time to a station that is suspended in mid-air.
A man painting a window so realistic that birds fly in and out of it all afternoon.
A staircase that leads to the ground floor from the ground floor, discovered with great excitement.
A gentleman whose hat continuously produces an orderly stream of pigeons he finds mildly inconvenient.
A cloud that follows one specific man around the city, refusing to rain on anyone else.
A librarian who checks out books to the people whose stories are inside them.
A city where every building is exactly the height of the person who lives inside it.
A fish that swims contentedly through the air of a sitting room, untroubled by the furniture.
A very punctual door that knocks on itself every morning at precisely seven o'clock.
A man who has successfully ironed all the weather for the week and hung it neatly in his wardrobe.
A crowd of umbrellas waiting patiently at a bus stop while the humans stand in the rain.
A mathematician who has proven that all staircases eventually lead to the sea.
A woman who earns a living by sorting the various shades of gray in overcast skies.
A cloud that, when it rains, produces miniature furniture instead of water.
A man who writes apology letters to all the socks separated in his laundry.
A woman rowing a boat through a forest of sleeping trees, her oars made of light.
A city that exists only between one breath and the next, its towers made of suspended water.
A staircase made of accumulated music, each step a chord held a moment too long.
A woman who is weaving a tapestry that shows the view from her window, in real time, in reverse.
A room where the furniture slowly rearranges itself whenever the occupant looks away.
A garden that blooms at night with flowers made of preserved memories.
A woman cooking soup in which all the flavors are colors and all the colors are emotions.
A corridor that grows longer the faster one walks down it, ending eventually in morning.
A scientist studying the migratory patterns of lost thoughts through empty rooms.
A woman reading a book so absorbing that the story begins to furnish the room around her.
A lighthouse beaming stories instead of light, each beam a different narrative arc.
A woman who travels by folding maps and stepping into the folds.
A soft rain that falls upward, lifting small objects back to wherever they were lost from.
A clock in a town square that measures not time but mood, its hands forever pointing at 'wistful'.
A door at the end of a long corridor that opens, gently, into the smell of bread from childhood.
A telescope that shows not distant space but distant futures, all of them pleasant.
A cat who has been secretly reorganizing a man's bookshelf by color for three years.
A sparrow who stole a single letter from every mailbox on the street and has assembled them into a poem.
A snail who crosses a busy intersection so confidently that all traffic simply waits.
A small dog who has convinced an entire household that he is in charge, with abundant evidence.
A crow who has taught itself to forge the signatures of famous composers on takeout menus.
A man who draws extremely detailed maps of rooms he has already left.
A magician whose only trick is producing one perfect grape from any hat, but does so with enormous gravitas.
A museum guard who very quietly adds one personal opinion label to every painting on his lunch break.
An inventor whose greatest creation is a machine for teaching manners to staircases.
A man who, out of politeness, has been pretending for twenty years not to notice that his hat is a fish.
A cat who sits in on a board meeting every week and is assumed by all to be the most senior member.
A woman who knocks on wood so frequently that the trees have started to wave back.
A chef so devoted to presentation that his restaurant has one dish and a three-year waiting list.
A child who has named every tree in her city and sends them birthday cards each spring.
A fox who tends a secret meadow in the heart of an industrial district, known only to the bees.
A girl who teaches her classroom of seeds to grow toward light through a song she invented.
A bear who leads a delegation of forest animals to a city hall meeting, waiting patiently for their agenda item.
A crow who drops acorns through the windows of government buildings with small notes attached.
A child who writes apology letters to rivers on behalf of the city that has polluted them.
A snail who has been appointed official witness to the slow return of a restored wetland.
An old woman who visits the last tree of a species every morning, refusing to let it be forgotten.
A boy who keeps a jar of clean mountain air and opens it at school to remind his classmates what it smells like.
A fox who maps the migration routes of butterflies and posts the maps on city telephone poles.
A group of children who have declared the vacant lot on their block a sovereign nation of wildflowers.
A woman who plants a tree for every meeting she attends about why trees cannot be planted.
A sparrow who carries seeds from one side of a wall to the other, indifferent to whose wall it is.
A cat who visits every household on a grieving street, spending exactly the right amount of time at each door.
A girl who organizes the birds of her neighborhood into a warning system, each species assigned a different alarm.
A traveling circus that appears in neighborhoods in crisis and performs free shows until things improve.
A group of grandmothers who show up to every protest with thermoses of tea and their own folding chairs.
A postman who tucks one handwritten encouraging note into every envelope containing official bad news.
A dog who walks a different lonely neighbor home every evening, choosing by some ineffable canine instinct.
A crow who carries messages between two communities that have stopped speaking, using small found objects as tokens.
A child who keeps a map of every neighbor who seems afraid and delivers drawings to those addresses.
A woman who bakes bread for every new family on the street before anyone else has thought to introduce themselves.
A snail who carries a tiny banner reading 'we are all still here' across the town square every single morning.
A group of children who have formed an official welcoming committee for anyone arriving in the neighborhood.
A flock of starlings that murmurs in the shape of the names of missing people above the city each dusk.
A woman who has been knitting a very long scarf for twenty years, adding a row for every act of community kindness she witnesses.
A crow who replaces official signs with more accurate ones and has never once been caught.
A child who reads the fine print of every document aloud at the dinner table until the adults start paying attention.
A fox who collects the things people say when they think no one is listening and arranges them into a small museum.
A librarian owl who flies through the city at night, leaving banned books on the windowsills of curious households.
A girl who keeps a notebook of every promise made by every official in her town and reads them back at inconvenient moments.
A parrot who has memorized the speeches of every politician she has heard and recites them back in the wrong order.
A tortoise who carries the complete history of a place on her shell in letters too small to read without getting very close.
A woman who embroiders the real names of things onto decorative cushions and leaves them in waiting rooms.
A crow who has been systematically replacing corporate logos with the names of the workers who made the products.
A child who asks every adult she meets the same three questions and keeps a meticulous record of the answers.
A dog who can smell when someone is lying and sits directly in front of them with an expression of patient disappointment.
A woman who translates bureaucratic letters into plain language and posts the translations beside the originals on community boards.
A woman who has been quietly correcting the historical plaques in her city with a very small chisel for fifteen years.
A small girl who has been rearranging the letters on the town notice board into more interesting messages since she could reach.
A rabbit who moves property survey stakes to slightly more equitable positions in the night.
A woman who grows the most magnificent garden in a neighborhood that has been told it is scheduled for demolition.
A crow who has memorized every loophole in the city ordinances and shares them freely with whoever needs one.
A child who decorates every surveillance camera on her street with tiny hand-knitted hats.
A fox who digs a tunnel under every new fence erected in her territory, as a matter of personal principle.
A very old tortoise who has quietly outlasted four governments and is not remotely concerned about the fifth.
A woman who plants bulbs in every place she has been told she is not permitted to plant bulbs.
A girl who has discovered that if she sings while doing something prohibited, nobody can quite bring themselves to stop her.
A pigeon who roosts on every newly installed anti-pigeon spike and has quietly organized the others to do the same.
A bear who sits in the road whenever a logging truck passes through the forest, unhurried and enormous.
A child who writes the word 'no' in chalk on every step of the town hall and returns to rewrite it every morning.
A woman who has grown a hedge so magnificent that the planning authority keeps postponing the demolition order just to look at it.
A crow who has appointed herself guardian of every elderly person on her street, monitoring them through windows with sharp professional concern.
A girl who has organized the neighborhood animals into a lost-and-found network, returning things at her own careful pace.
A fox who runs an informal counseling service for woodland creatures out of a hollow log, by appointment only.
A woman who leaves the light on in her front window every night so the street never feels dark to anyone walking home late.
A child who remembers the birthday of every person in her building and slides a hand-drawn card under each door.
A cat who sits with whoever in the household is most anxious, choosing by an instinct the household has come to trust completely.
A snail who delivers tiny encouraging notes to other snails who appear to be struggling with the pace of things.
A grandmother who has never met a child who didn't need feeding and acts on this knowledge without exception.
A woman who has turned her front garden into a place where anyone can sit quietly for as long as they need.
A bear who runs a library of stories told aloud, for anyone who cannot read or is simply too tired to.
A child who carries a spare umbrella at all times for the eventuality of an unprepared stranger, which occurs more than you'd think.
